summary: Kenneth Suna documents a covered call series where he aims to make $180/week per position by selling calls on stocks like HIMS (~$50/share). He demonstrates that the strategy is scalable across multiple positions with different capital amounts, generating ~$1,300+ weekly across a $27k portfolio, debunking TikTok claims that only high-volatility stocks like Tesla ($900/week) make this strategy viable.
takeaways: Target $180/week per covered call on HIMS stock (~$50/share cost)
Don't need $27k total; can start with 1-3 positions if capital-constrained
High-volatility stocks like Tesla require $100+ shares (expensive entry); focus on more accessible stocks with decent premiums instead
Weekly adjustments planned as positions get assigned or expire
Realistic expectation: $80-322/week per position depending on capital deployed

strategy_candidates.change: Diversify across 5-7 lower-priced stocks ($50-150/share) instead of concentrating on fewer high-volatility names to reduce single-position risk while maintaining $1,250/mo target
strategy_candidates.evidence: Kenneth demonstrates $1,300+/week from HIMS, Aqua, Joby, Nphase, and other positions; shows each position generates different premiums ($80-$322/week). This multi-stock approach reduces assignment risk and volatility compared to concentrated bets on Tesla-like names.

title: $1000 a week because of tiktok
